description:
Trigger: user reads secretx-pkcs11/README.md line 10-16, sees the documented URI 'secretx:pkcs11:<slot>/<label>[?lib=<path>&pin=<pin>]' with bullet 'pin — token PIN; defaults to PKCS11_PIN env var', and constructs a URI like 'secretx:pkcs11:0/key?lib=...&pin=1234'.

Bad behavior: from_uri (secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:80-88, 118) only reads ?lib= from the URI. The pin URI param is silently ignored. user_pin is sourced exclusively from std::env::var("PKCS11_PIN"). Caller's URI-supplied PIN is dropped without an error, login fails, and the user sees 'CKR_USER_NOT_LOGGED_IN' with no clue why their PIN was discarded.

Two valid fixes — pick one and make code+README consistent:
(a) Remove ?pin= from the README. Justification: PINs in URIs are a security smell — URIs end up in logs, error messages, ps output, and config files committed to git. Env var or short-lived process secret is correct. The lib.rs doc comment at line 4-12 is already correct and only mentions PKCS11_LIB and PKCS11_PIN env. The README is the outlier.
(b) Implement ?pin= in from_uri to match the README, with a loud security warning in the rustdoc.

Recommended: (a). Update README, add a 'Security' section explaining why PIN is env-only.
title: README documents ?pin= URI param that the code does not implement
